evals.prompt: I need to upgrade my Next.js project from version 13 to 15.
evals.expected_output: Incremental upgrade — never skip majors: (1) v13 -> v14 first: run npx @next/codemod@latest upgrade ., test, fix issues. (2) v14 -> v15: run codemods again. Key v15 changes: cookies(), headers(), params are now Promises — must await. Fetch defaults to no-store. Update React to 19 for Next.js 15 compatibility.

evals.prompt: After upgrading to Next.js 15, all my pages are showing stale cached data.
evals.expected_output: Breaking change: Next.js 15 defaults fetch to no-store (no caching) instead of force-cache. Audit all fetch calls — add cache: 'force-cache' where you want SSG behavior, or revalidate: N for ISR. This is the opposite of v14 default. Run next build to see all dynamic route warnings.

evals.prompt: I want to skip directly from Next.js 12 to 15 to save time.
evals.expected_output: Violation: No major version skipping. Upgrade one major at a time: 12 -> 13, 13 -> 14, 14 -> 15. Each major has different codemods and breaking changes. Skipping causes compounding failures that are hard to debug. Run npx @next/codemod@latest for each step. Test after each increment with next dev and next build.

should_not_trigger: Write a custom hook for data fetching
Configure ESLint rules for the team
